title: Canonical Property Profile
description: v1 of the one canonical property-profile contract (PRD section 32.3). All connectors, rules, scenarios, reports, and UI views must exchange this contract; modules may not invent competing property schemas. Every fact value carries a REQUIRED provenance_ref that must resolve to a provenance record in this profile (PRD sections 9 and 19; fix for M0-T004 G3 defect D1). Referential integrity of EVERY provenance_ref site (fact values, zoning.mapped_features, and the v1.1 district-provenance maps) is enforced by .github/scripts/validate_contracts.py for fixtures and must be enforced by the backend for live data. CONTRACT 1.1.0 (task M1-T006, additive; M1-T005 G3 adjudication #1 + defects D2/D4): documents the three keys the accepted M1-T005 API already emits (OPTIONAL per-fact coverage_status, OPTIONAL top-level data_completeness, OPTIONAL top-level reproducibility) and adds OPTIONAL machine-resolvable district-provenance maps under zoning. Every 1.0.0 instance remains valid: no required key was added, removed, or retyped. CONTRACT 1.2.0 (task M2-T004, additive; owner code-audit P1 2026-07-17): adds the OPTIONAL top-level status_dimensions object (five INDEPENDENT status dimensions - source_record_completeness, analysis_readiness, rule_coverage, geometry_validity, financial_readiness - never collapsed into one label, per PRD s12 and GENERATIVE_DEVELOPMENT_STRATEGY_REQUIREMENTS s3.3; dimensions the platform cannot yet compute are declared 'not_computed', never inferred), the OPTIONAL missing_inputs[].feasibility_relevant flag (membership in the documented feasibility-relevant completeness basis - data_completeness and source_record_completeness are derived from feasibility-relevant gaps ONLY, never from all 108 PLUTO columns), OPTIONAL reproducibility.response_digest + reproducibility.digest_canonicalization, and the four OPTIONAL source_fact identity/lineage keys (fact_key, observation_id, value_digest, response_digest - see source_fact.schema.json). Every 1.0.0 and 1.1.0 instance remains valid: all new keys are optional and no existing key changed. CONTRACT 1.3.0 (task M2-T006, additive; M1-T009 G1 findings D1/D2 + M2-T002 G3 finding N3): adds the OPTIONAL typed reproducibility.staleness object (machine-readable cache/last-known-good serve visibility - the successor to the served_from_last_known_good connector-note convention, which is retained for humans), refreshes the correlation_id description to match cache/LKG-era generation semantics, and documents the open-schema zoning.mapped_features and conflict keys the accepted builder emits. Every 1.0.0, 1.1.0, and 1.2.0 instance remains valid: all new keys are optional and no existing key changed. CONTRACT 1.4.0 (task M2-T012, additive; owner directive 2026-07-20 - ONE coordinated wave-connector + spatial-intersection integration, no successive contract versions): adds three OPTIONAL top-level keys that carry the accepted connector-wave and spatial-intersection facts into the canonical profile with full provenance - zoning_features (per-layer retrieval facts for the six official DCP GIS zoning-features layers, task M2-T007; CITYWIDE reference data, explicitly NOT lot-level determinations), lot_geometry (per-BBL MapPLUTO tax-lot geometry facts, task M2-T009; the geometry-validity taxonomy is preserved and it is never a legal boundary certification), and spatial_intersection (the task M2-T013 lot/zoning facts-with-uncertainty substrate - exact geometric results, boundary distances, split-share RANGES, positional-uncertainty classes, and professional-review flags - with uncertainty NEVER collapsed into a definitive single-district assignment and no Verified label, deliberately EXCLUDING the engine-internal coverage_audits diagnostic). Every provenance_ref / provenance_refs in the three new keys resolves to a provenance record in this profile's provenance array (the backend enforces this for live data via app.profile.builder._assert_provenance_integrity; extending .github/scripts/validate_contracts.py to the three new fixture sites is a recommended follow-up, out of this task's file scope). The fourth (geometric) evidence stream of the task M2-T008 cross-check surfaces geometric-vs-ZTLDB disagreement through the EXISTING conflicts array - no new conflict shape. Every 1.0.0, 1.1.0, 1.2.0, and 1.3.0 instance remains valid: all three new keys are optional and no existing key changed.

profile_version.description: Version of this profile instance (monotonic per property) plus the contract version used.

contract_version.description: CLOSED enum of published contract versions within the v1 major line. 1.0.0 = M0-T009 shape; 1.1.0 = M1-T006 additive extension; 1.2.0 = M2-T004 additive extension (status_dimensions, feasibility_relevant, digests, fact identity/lineage); 1.3.0 = M2-T006 additive extension (typed reproducibility.staleness; correlation_id description refresh; open-schema key documentation); 1.4.0 = M2-T012 additive extension (the three OPTIONAL top-level wave/spatial-integration keys zoning_features, lot_geometry, and spatial_intersection). New versions are admitted only by an accepted contract task that appends to this enum; arbitrary versions are rejected. DECLARATION SEMANTICS (input to M2-T003, deliberately not preempted here): as with 1.1.0, adding 1.2.0 to this enum publishes the version; WHICH version a producer declares (the accepted M1-T005 builder still declares 1.0.0 while emitting the additive keys, all legal at every version because the keys are optional) and whether declared version and emitted key set are validated against each other is the M2-T003 validation/typegen decision.

bins.description: Building Identification Numbers on the lot.

borough.description: Normalized borough display name (documented official spellings; see common.schema.json for grounding). Raw connector spellings are preserved in provenance original_value.

geometry.description: GeoJSON geometry of the lot. OPEN-WITH-FLAG: the full tax-lot geometry contract (CRS handling, polygon validity, geometry versioning) is deferred to the M2 tax-lot geometry tasks. Note from research (docs/research/M0-T002-geoclient-address-resolution.md section 2.6): Geoclient x/y are NAD83 New York-Long Island state plane in feet, lat/long are derived; the EPSG mapping is UNKNOWN from official pages and must be verified before geometry import.

lot_facts.description: Lot-level facts (area, frontage, depth, corner status, etc.). Material by definition: every fact value REQUIRES a provenance_ref (PRD sections 9/19).

existing_building_facts.description: Existing-building facts (floor area, stories, use, landmark status, etc.). Every fact value REQUIRES a provenance_ref.

zoning.description: Zoning districts, overlays, special districts, and mapped features. OPEN-WITH-FLAG: district-code enums/patterns are NOT modeled in v1 because the official zoning source family has not yet been researched (M0-T002 covered address/BBL/BIN only; Geoclient examples expose no zoning district designation field - research doc section 2.6). Free strings here must carry provenance; enum grounding is added additively when the M2 zoning-source research lands.

items.description: DELIBERATELY OPEN object (no structural tightening at 1.3.0; consumers runtime-narrow - the M2-T002 client helpers are the accepted pattern). DOCUMENTED KEYS (M2-T002 G3 finding N3) that the accepted builder emits per mapped zoning/regulatory feature: 'feature' (string; the source column name from the builder's mapped-feature column set, e.g. 'splitzone', 'landmark', 'firm07_flag'), plus the fact_value keys 'value' (any JSON value, verbatim-normalized), 'provenance_ref' (string; MUST resolve to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array - enforced by validate_contracts.py for fixtures and by the backend for live data), 'coverage_status' (coverage_status.schema.json value), and 'units' (string, present only when the value has units). The shape stays open so future mapped-feature sources (M2 GIS layers, landmark/flood connectors) can add keys without a contract bump; this description is the contract-level record of the emitted keys, not a structural constraint.

district_provenance.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0, OPTIONAL (M1-T005 G3 defect D4): machine-resolvable provenance for zoning.districts values. Each key MUST be a value present in the sibling districts array; each mapped provenance_ref MUST resolve to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array. Both rules are enforced by validate_contracts.py for fixtures and by the backend for live data. The plain-string districts array is unchanged and remains the canonical value list; this map only annotates it, so 1.0.0 instances (no map) stay valid.

commercial_overlay_provenance.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0, OPTIONAL (G3 D4): same contract as district_provenance, keyed by values of the sibling commercial_overlays array.

special_district_provenance.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0, OPTIONAL (G3 D4): same contract as district_provenance, keyed by values of the sibling special_districts array.

project_intent.description: User-selected development objectives for the analysis. Objective identifiers are finalized with the scenario-engine contracts (M5); PRD section 6 is the source list.

provenance.description: Source provenance records for every fact used (PRD section 9). Each record follows the canonical source_fact contract. Every fact_value.provenance_ref in this profile must equal some provenance_id in this array.

missing_inputs.description: Inputs the platform could not obtain, with criticality (PRD section 12 data-completeness). Every absent/unknown source column stays listed here (unknown is stated, never hidden - PRD section 9); CONTRACT 1.2.0 adds the OPTIONAL feasibility_relevant flag so consumers can tell which gaps drive the completeness statuses.

feasibility_relevant.description: CONTRACT 1.2.0, OPTIONAL (task M2-T004, owner P1 bullet 1): true when this field is a member of the documented feasibility-relevant completeness basis (the builder's FEASIBILITY_COLUMNS set, grounded in the official PLUTO 26v1 data dictionary via docs/research/pluto-mappluto-2026-07-16.md). data_completeness and status_dimensions.source_record_completeness are derived ONLY from entries with feasibility_relevant true - absence of a column outside the basis (e.g. an administrative-district column, or a conditional-presence zoning column whose SODA null-omission absence is not distinguishable from 'none') never degrades completeness. This replaces the pre-1.2.0 behavior in which all 108 possible PLUTO columns fed the derivation. Entries without this flag (pre-1.2.0 producers) are treated as feasibility-relevant by consumers that must assume the old derivation.

conflicts.description: Cross-source conflicts detected for facts on this property. Conflicts must remain visible in results and reports (PRD sections 2/9).

items.description: One cross-source (or intra-source) conflict. DOCUMENTED OPEN KEY (M2-T002 G3 finding N3): the accepted builder additionally emits 'reason' (string or null; the connector's explanation of why the values disagree, e.g. an identifier-consistency check message). It stays structurally undeclared at 1.3.0 - additional properties remain permitted - and consumers read it through runtime narrowing, tolerating absence.

One conflicting value. DOCUMENTED OPEN KEY (M2-T002 G3 finding N3): the accepted builder additionally emits 'derivation' (string; how this value was obtained, e.g. 'derived from the canonical BBL digits' vs a verbatim record field). It stays structurally undeclared at 1.3.0 and is read via runtime narrowing; absence is always tolerated.

resolution.description: PLATFORM-DEFINED enum (not a government-source value): grounded in PRD section 5 (user confirms/corrects), section 9 (user confirmed/overrode), and section 8 (official source priority order). 'source_priority' means the platform preferred the higher-priority official source and the conflict remains visible.

user_confirmations.description: User confirmations and overrides of official facts (PRD sections 5/9).

data_completeness.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0, OPTIONAL (M1-T005 G3 defect D2): profile-level data-completeness status - exactly the 3 PRD section 12 values, referenced from coverage_status.schema.json (never duplicated inline). Derived deterministically from missing_inputs criticality. DERIVATION BASIS FIXED AT 1.2.0 (task M2-T004, owner P1): only FEASIBILITY-RELEVANT missing inputs count (entries with feasibility_relevant true - the documented FEASIBILITY_COLUMNS basis): any critical feasibility-relevant missing input -> missing_critical; any feasibility-relevant missing input -> missing_noncritical; else complete. Before 1.2.0 the derivation counted every absent column of the 108-column PLUTO inventory, which made 'complete' unreachable in practice (the owner-audit defect). The key, its enum, and its placement are unchanged for v1.1 consumers.

reproducibility.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0, OPTIONAL (M1-T005 G3 defect D2): retrieval metadata that lets a report reader reproduce this profile build (PRD section 9: retain the exact source snapshot used; PRD section 20 item 17: reproducibility identifier). Shape matches the accepted M1-T005 builder output exactly: the builder emits every 1.1.0 subfield unconditionally, so the 10 original subfields are REQUIRED when the object is present. CONTRACT 1.2.0 (task M2-T004) adds two OPTIONAL snapshot-lineage subfields - response_digest and digest_canonicalization - which the current builder also emits unconditionally but which stay optional in the schema so 1.1.0 instances remain valid. CONTRACT 1.3.0 (task M2-T006) adds the OPTIONAL staleness subfield - the typed machine-readable serve-freshness channel (M1-T009 G1 D2) - also emitted unconditionally by the 1.3.0 builder but optional in the schema so 1.1.0/1.2.0 instances remain valid. Single-source (PLUTO) shape; when M2 merges multiple sources this evolves additively (e.g. a per-source array ALONGSIDE this object), never by retyping these keys.

correlation_id.description: Correlation id of the request whose SUCCESSFUL UPSTREAM RETRIEVAL produced this payload - the reproducibility identifier joining this profile to the retrieval logs and job records that actually built it (PRD section 25 structured logs; PRD section 20 item 17). DESCRIPTION REFRESHED AT 1.3.0 (M1-T009 G1 finding D1, adjudication J1): on a freshly built profile this equals the X-Correlation-ID response header of the serving request; on a cache or last-known-good serve (see the sibling staleness object) it deliberately remains the ORIGINAL fetch's id - the only value that truthfully joins to the retrieval that produced the payload - while the X-Correlation-ID header identifies the serving HTTP exchange. Rewriting it to the serving request's id would falsify reproducibility.

source_id.description: Source-registry id of the official source used (PRD sections 8.2/9), e.g. 'nyc-dcp-pluto-soda'.

dataset_id.description: Official dataset identifier within the source (PRD section 9 'dataset version or page/document version' context), e.g. SODA id '64uk-42ks'.

dataset_version.description: Dataset version used for this build, e.g. PLUTO release '26v1' (PRD section 9). REQUIRED key, explicitly null when the source publishes/exposes none at retrieval time (same visible-absence pattern as source_fact.effective_date); never an empty string.

request_url.description: Exact official request URL used, so the retrieval can be repeated against the same endpoint (PRD section 9 provenance; secrets/tokens are never part of this URL - they travel in headers).

retrieved_at.description: Timestamp of the successful source retrieval this profile was built from (PRD section 9 retrieved timestamp).

record_count.description: Number of source records the retrieval returned (PRD section 9 snapshot completeness; >1 signals an unexpected multi-record response kept visible rather than silently truncated).

drift_signals.description: Connector schema-drift signals observed during retrieval (PRD section 25 schema-drift alerts), e.g. 'non_finite_number_value:lotarea'. Facts named by drift signals carry coverage_status 'unsupported'. Empty array = no drift.

connector_notes.description: Connector notes carrying official data-dictionary semantics for present-but-unknown values (e.g. 'yearbuilt_unknown: ...'); feeds missing_inputs. Conflicts and stale data stay visible (PRD principle 4).

coverage_policy.description: Verbatim statement of the deterministic policy that produced every coverage_status in this profile (PRD section 12: AI/connector confidence never substitutes for legal-review status). Stored per profile so a historical report explains its own labels even after the policy text evolves.

response_digest.description: CONTRACT 1.2.0, OPTIONAL (task M2-T004, owner P1 bullets 3-4): canonical digest of the ENTIRE parsed source response body this profile was built from (canonical-json-1 form; see common.schema.json digest_sha256). Deterministic across identical responses; any source value change flips it. Equals the response_digest carried on every provenance record of this build, closing the snapshot lineage: observation (observation_id, retrieved_at) -> source version (dataset_version) -> request (request_url) -> exact response content (this digest).

digest_canonicalization.description: CONTRACT 1.2.0, OPTIONAL (task M2-T004): verbatim statement of the canonicalization rules under which response_digest and every value_digest in this profile were computed (spec id + key order + whitespace + encoding + number handling). Stored per profile - like coverage_policy - so a historical report can recompute and verify its own digests even after the canonicalization spec evolves.

staleness.description: CONTRACT 1.3.0, OPTIONAL (task M2-T006; M1-T009 G1 findings D1/D2): typed, machine-readable freshness of the serve that produced this payload - the first-class successor to the 'served_from_last_known_good:' connector-note convention (the human-readable note in connector_notes is RETAINED alongside this object; conflicts and stale data stay visible, PRD principle 4). ABSENCE SEMANTICS: when this object is ABSENT the payload came from a pre-1.3.0 producer - consumers MUST NOT infer freshness from absence and must fall back to the connector-note convention plus the truthful retrieved_at. A 1.3.0 builder emits this object on EVERY serve (a fresh build emits {served_from_cache: false, stale: false} with no other keys - the same optional-in-schema-but-unconditionally-emitted pattern as 1.2.0's response_digest). PRESENCE SEMANTICS: served_from_cache true means the payload was served from the TTL cache or the last-known-good store rather than a fresh upstream retrieval, and then original_retrieved_at and age_seconds are REQUIRED; stale true means the serve fell back to last-known-good data BECAUSE the upstream retrieval failed, and then upstream_error_type is REQUIRED and served_from_cache must be true (a within-TTL cache hit is served_from_cache true + stale false). Values are copied verbatim from the serving fetcher's own record of the serve - never invented, never derived from wall-clock guesses. NOTE for TS consumers: the conditional requirements are enforced at JSON-Schema validation level (allOf/anyOf/const); the generated TypeScript types carry the two required booleans and three optional fields without the cross-field conditions.

served_from_cache.description: True when this payload was served from the TTL cache or the last-known-good store instead of a fresh upstream retrieval (M1-T009 G1 D2: a within-TTL cache serve now carries an explicit marker). False on a freshly built profile.

stale.description: True when the serve fell back to the last-known-good snapshot because the upstream retrieval FAILED (the response is known-stale official data, loudly labeled). False on a fresh build and on a within-TTL cache hit (bounded by the cache TTL and the truthful retrieved_at).

upstream_error_type.description: REQUIRED when stale is true: the typed error_type of the upstream failure that forced the last-known-good serve (e.g. 'source_unavailable', 'timeout', 'rate_limited' - the connector's documented error taxonomy). Absent on fresh builds and within-TTL cache hits (no failure occurred; never invented).

original_retrieved_at.description: REQUIRED when served_from_cache is true: timestamp of the ORIGINAL successful retrieval the served snapshot came from. Always equals the sibling reproducibility.retrieved_at (which is never rewritten to serve time - M1-T009 provenance rule); duplicated here so the staleness object is self-contained for consumers and logs.

age_seconds.description: REQUIRED when served_from_cache is true: age of the served snapshot at serve time in seconds, measured by the serving process's monotonic clock (injected in tests - deterministic, never a wall-clock guess). Absent on fresh builds (age of a fresh retrieval is not a meaningful serve-staleness signal).

allOf.$comment: served_from_cache true => original_retrieved_at + age_seconds are required (a cached serve must state what it served and how old it was).
stale true => upstream_error_type is required AND served_from_cache is true (staleness only arises from serving stored data after an upstream failure).

status_dimensions.description: CONTRACT 1.2.0, OPTIONAL (task M2-T004; owner code-audit P1 bullet 1; PRD section 12; GENERATIVE_DEVELOPMENT_STRATEGY_REQUIREMENTS section 3.3): five INDEPENDENT status dimensions of this profile. They are never collapsed into one confidence label, and a dimension the platform cannot yet compute is declared 'not_computed' - never inferred, never invented, never hidden. All six subfields are REQUIRED when the object is present because the builder emits each unconditionally (same pattern as reproducibility): omitting a dimension would silently collapse the dimension set. Enum values are the CLOSED set the current deterministic backend can emit; future capabilities (M2 geometry validation, M4 rule engine, financial engine) extend these enums ADDITIVELY via an accepted contract task - values are never removed. The legacy top-level data_completeness key is unchanged and continues to serve v1.1 consumers; source_record_completeness is the successor dimension, not a replacement.

source_record_completeness.description: Completeness of the official source record RELATIVE TO THE DOCUMENTED FEASIBILITY-RELEVANT COLUMN BASIS ONLY (builder FEASIBILITY_COLUMNS, grounded in the official PLUTO 26v1 data dictionary; see packages/contracts/README.md and docs/research/pluto-mappluto-2026-07-16.md). 'complete' = every basis column is present with a usable value; 'partial' = at least one basis column is absent, officially-unknown (e.g. YearBuilt 0 per dictionary p.34-35), or drift-flagged; 'not_computed' = the profile was built without a column inventory to judge against. Absence of the ~89 non-basis PLUTO columns NEVER degrades this dimension (the pre-1.2.0 108-column defect). Says nothing about geometry, rules, or finance - those are the other dimensions.

analysis_readiness.description: Whether the DATA in this profile suffices to begin deterministic rules evaluation. Evaluated in documented order: (1) 'blocked_data_conflict' when an unresolved conflict touches an identity field (bbl/borocode/block/lot) or a critical feasibility column; (2) 'blocked_missing_critical' when a critical feasibility column (lotarea, zonedist1) is absent or carries an unusable drift-flagged value; (3) else 'ready'. 'ready' is a DATA statement only - it is NOT the PRD section 32.1 'ready_for_analysis' workflow state and never implies that user confirmation (PRD section 5) occurred; the backend state machine consumes this dimension and still owns the workflow.

rule_coverage.description: Coverage of this profile by published deterministic rules. 'not_computed' is the ONLY value the platform may emit before the M4 rule engine exists: rule applicability is not yet computable, and declaring anything else would invent coverage (PRD section 12: no result is 'verified' without a published rule + reviewer pipeline). M4 extends this enum additively (an accepted contract task appends values; this single-value enum is deliberate, not a placeholder omission).

geometry_validity.description: Validity of the lot geometry attached to this profile. 'missing' = the official source provided no usable geometry (no finite coordinate pair), stated explicitly rather than silently omitting the geometry key; 'not_computed' = geometry is present (currently a PLUTO-derived point; dictionary p.39-40 / EPSG:2263 research note) but the geometry-validation pipeline does not exist until the M2 tax-lot geometry tasks - validity is unknown and is NOT asserted. 'valid'/'invalid'/'repaired' values land additively with that pipeline. Independent of source_record_completeness by construction: geometry columns are excluded from the completeness basis.

financial_readiness.description: Readiness of financial-feasibility evaluation for this profile. 'not_computed' is the ONLY value before a financial-evaluation engine exists (GENERATIVE_DEVELOPMENT_STRATEGY_REQUIREMENTS section 3.3 financial status; Phase C evaluators). Future values (e.g. assumption-based estimate states) land additively with that engine via an accepted contract task.

policy.description: Verbatim statement of the deterministic derivation policy that produced the five dimension values in this profile, including the completeness basis identification. Stored per profile - same self-description pattern as reproducibility.coverage_policy - so a historical report explains its own labels after the policy evolves.

zoning_features.description: CONTRACT 1.4.0, OPTIONAL (task M2-T012): per-layer RETRIEVAL facts for the six official DCP GIS zoning-features layers (nyzd districts, nyco commercial overlays, nysp special-purpose districts, nysp_sd special-purpose subdistricts, nylh limited-height districts, nyzma zoning-map amendments; task M2-T007). These are CITYWIDE reference-data provenance facts - which layers were consulted, at what content digest, when, and in which CRS - NOT lot-level zoning determinations (the official use limitation is explicit: 'these features are not intended for determining zoning at the individual tax lot level'; lot-level assignment with uncertainty lives in spatial_intersection). DELIBERATELY OPEN: the per-layer entry carries at least layer + provenance_ref and the accepted connector also passes through record_count, normalized_digest, source_data_last_edited, crs, and drift_signals; the shape stays open so future layer facts can be added without a contract bump. OPTIONAL so every 1.0.0-1.3.0 instance stays valid.

layers.description: One entry per zoning-features layer consulted for this profile's analysis.

items.description: DELIBERATELY OPEN per-layer retrieval record. DOCUMENTED KEYS the accepted builder emits: 'layer' (one of nyzd|nyco|nysp|nysp_sd|nylh|nyzma), 'provenance_ref' (string; MUST resolve to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array - enforced by the backend for live data), 'coverage_status' (coverage_status.schema.json value; 'conditional' for an unreviewed official retrieval, 'unsupported' when the retrieval carried a schema-drift signal), plus open pass-through keys 'record_count', 'normalized_digest', 'source_data_last_edited', 'crs', and 'drift_signals'. The shape stays open (no structural tightening) so additional zoning-features facts can be added without a contract bump.

layer.description: Canonical zoning-features layer key: nyzd | nyco | nysp | nysp_sd | nylh | nyzma.

provenance_ref.description: REQUIRED provenance record backing this layer retrieval. Must resolve to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array (PRD sections 9/19).

coverage_status.description: PRD section 12 coverage status of this layer retrieval - never derived from connector confidence; never 'verified' before a published-rule/reviewer pipeline exists.

lot_geometry.description: CONTRACT 1.4.0, OPTIONAL (task M2-T012): per-BBL MapPLUTO tax-lot geometry facts (task M2-T009) with full provenance. The connector's geometry-validity taxonomy is preserved verbatim (outcome + geometry_status + review flags + separate original/normalized digests); this is deterministic geometry provenance, NEVER a legal boundary certification and never a zoning determination. DELIBERATELY OPEN: carries at least outcome + provenance_ref and the accepted builder also passes through review_required, geometry_status, area_sq_ft, shape_area_attribute_sq_ft, normalized_digest, original_geometry_digest, crs, source_data_last_edited, shapely_version/geos_version, and drift_signals. OPTIONAL so every 1.0.0-1.3.0 instance stays valid.

outcome.description: Explicit per-lot outcome: 'single_feature' | 'no_feature' | 'multiple_features' (multiple features for one BBL is review-required, never a silent first-pick - task M2-T009 safeguard 1).

review_required.description: True when the lot geometry cannot be safely used without professional review (multiple features, invalid/uncharacterizable geometry, or identifier conflict).

geometry_status.description: Geometry-validity state of the single feature: 'valid' | 'repaired' | 'invalid_geometry' | 'review_required'; null when there is no single feature (no_feature/multiple_features).

area_sq_ft.description: Planar lot area in square feet computed ONLY in the validated EPSG:2263 projected CRS; null when no usable geometry exists. Never computed from degrees.

provenance_ref.description: REQUIRED provenance record backing this lot-geometry fact. Must resolve to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array (PRD sections 9/19).

coverage_status.description: PRD section 12 coverage status of the lot-geometry fact - never derived from connector confidence; never 'verified' before a published-rule/reviewer pipeline exists.

crs.description: Authoritative source CRS stamp (EPSG:2263 / wkid 102718). Open object; coordinates are never interpreted outside this validated CRS.

spatial_intersection.description: CONTRACT 1.4.0, OPTIONAL (task M2-T012): the task M2-T013 lot/zoning spatial-intersection substrate - deterministic FACTS-WITH-UNCERTAINTY only. It carries the exact geometric results (raw/firm/dilated intersection areas, boundary distances), the split-share RANGES (share_min/share_point/share_max, never renormalized), the positional-uncertainty classes (pair_class and lot_overall_class), and the professional-review flags - and it NEVER collapses an uncertain boundary into a definitive single-district assignment, NEVER carries a Verified label (Verified is exclusively an M4 published-rule + G6 professional outcome), and DELIBERATELY EXCLUDES the engine-internal coverage_audits diagnostic (owner amendment invariant 6). The permanent coverage_note states this substrate is not a Verified zoning determination. DELIBERATELY OPEN so the full engine record passes through without a contract bump. OPTIONAL so every 1.0.0-1.3.0 instance stays valid.

bbl.description: BBL this intersection substrate describes; equals the profile identity.bbl.

lot_overall_class.description: Lot-overall positional-uncertainty class: 'single_district_confident' | 'split_lot_confident' | 'boundary_uncertain' | 'sliver_ambiguous' | 'data_conflict' | 'invalid_geometry_review'. An uncertainty CLASSIFICATION over base zoning, NEVER a definitive single-district assignment and never a Verified determination.

professional_review_required.description: True when any positional uncertainty, coverage anomaly, ZTLDB set-conflict, or non-intersectable geometry routes this lot to professional review (task M2-T013 fail-safe rollup).

coverage_note.description: The permanent honest coverage note stamped on every intersection record: facts-with-uncertainty, NOT a Verified zoning determination (Verified requires an M4 published rule and G6 professional approval).

provenance_refs.description: REQUIRED non-empty list of provenance records this intersection was computed from (the derived-result record and its lot-geometry / zoning-features inputs). Every entry MUST resolve to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array (PRD sections 9/19).

pairs.description: Per (lot, district) intersection records. DELIBERATELY OPEN items: each carries the exact geometric facts (raw_intersection_sq_ft, firm_intersection_sq_ft, dilated_intersection_sq_ft, distance_ft, lot_area_sq_ft), the share RANGE (share_min/share_point/share_max, minor_portion), and the pair_class uncertainty label - the class is the interpretation, the numbers are the facts, and the range is never collapsed to a point.

items.description: Open (lot, district) intersection record; consumers runtime-narrow. Never a Verified assignment.

crosscheck.description: Open ZTLDB cross-check outcome (agreement/ordering/set-conflict/absent) with the geometric-vs-ZTLDB ordered district sets; a ZTLDB agreement upgrades a geometrically uncertain DISPLAYED result to 'conditional' at most - never confident/Verified. null when no cross-check was computed.

review_reasons.description: Explicit reasons this lot routes to professional review; empty when none.

unassigned_area.description: Per-family explicit unassigned (coverage-gap) area records; never renormalized away.

overlap_area.description: Per-family, same-family-only overlap area records; never computed across families.

accuracy_records.description: Every input positional-accuracy stamp (documented vs assumed tolerances) used in the intersection.

policy.description: Open snapshot of the deterministic intersection policy (version + the owner-approved advisory parameters) under which this record was computed, so a historical report explains its own uncertainty rules.

notes.description: Visible uncertainty notes (e.g. split-share ranges must feed M4 rules as ranges); never conflicts, never adjudications.

provenance_ref.description: REQUIRED id of the provenance record backing this fact (PRD section 19: a material calculation can never be exported without provenance). Must resolve to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array.

coverage_status.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0, OPTIONAL (M1-T005 G3 defect D2): PRD section 12 coverage status of this fact - exactly the 6 canonical values, referenced from coverage_status.schema.json (never duplicated inline). OPTIONAL so every 1.0.0 instance stays valid. Derived only from review status and conflict/drift state, never from connector confidence, and never 'verified' before a published-rule/reviewer pipeline exists (see reproducibility.coverage_policy).

provenance_ref_list.description: Non-empty list of provenance_ref ids; every entry must resolve to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array (PRD sections 9/19). A list (not a single ref) so one value can be corroborated by multiple provenance records - e.g. PLUTO zonedist columns today plus the M2 DCP GIS zoning-features cross-check later - without another contract bump, and so duplicate source columns can never silently drop a record.

district_provenance_map.description: CONTRACT 1.1.0 (G3 D4): map from a zoning value string (a member of the sibling plain-string array) to the provenance records backing it. Two validator-enforced integrity rules: (1) every key is present in the sibling value array; (2) every listed provenance_ref resolves to a provenance_id in this profile's provenance array. The map may cover a subset of the array (partial linkage is legal; absent linkage means provenance is joinable only via provenance[].original_field_name, the documented 1.0.0 situation).
